cases.title: Academic Admin creates term as first step of academic structure setup
preconditions: AcademicAdmin user is authenticated with institution scope.
No existing term with code 2026-1 in the test institution.
steps: Call POST /v1/terms with code, name, startDate, endDate, and isActive true using AcademicAdmin bearer token and Idempotency-Key.
Call GET /v1/terms and locate the newly created term by code.
Verify the term can be referenced as termId when creating a class section via POST /v1/class-sections.
cases.expected: Term is created and listable; FLOW-09 academic structure setup can proceed with the new term as the semester container for class sections.

cases.title: M02 Academic Structure persists term row and enforces class_section term FK
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for terms and class_sections tables.
AcademicAdmin auth context is available to the academic structure service.
steps: Create a term through the M02 academic structure module or POST /v1/terms API path.
Query the terms table and assert code, name, startDate, endDate, and isActive match the request.
Create a class section bound to the term termId and assert class_sections.term_id FK references the term row.
Attempt to create a class section with a non-existent termId and observe rejection.
cases.expected: Term row is committed with all required attributes; class sections must reference exactly one valid term via FK; invalid termId is rejected without orphan section rows.

cases.title: HTTP contract for POST /v1/terms create returns 200 with standard envelope
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
Request body includes code, name, startDate, endDate, and isActive per API design §4.2.
steps: Send POST /v1/terms with Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error}.
Assert data contains id, code, name, startDate, endDate, and isActive fields consistent with the request.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with standard envelope; data reflects the created term attributes and meta includes requestId and timestamp.

cases.title: HTTP contract for GET /v1/terms list returns paginated term collection
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
At least one term exists in the institution scope.
steps: Send GET /v1/terms with Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard envelope with data array.
Assert meta.pagination includes page, pageSize, totalItems, and totalPages per API conventions §2.5.
cases.expected: List response is HTTP 200 with paginated data array of terms and complete pagination metadata in meta.pagination.

cases.title: Term list pagination honors page and pageSize boundaries
preconditions: Database seed contains more than 25 terms for pagination testing.
AcademicAdmin auth context is established.
steps: Call GET /v1/terms?page=1&pageSize=25 and record totalItems and row count.
Call GET /v1/terms?page=2&pageSize=25 and assert a disjoint result set from page 1.
Request a page beyond totalPages and assert empty data array with consistent totalItems.
cases.expected: Pagination returns correct page boundaries, stable totalItems across pages, and an empty data array when page exceeds totalPages without cross-scope count leakage.

cases.title: Academic Admin updates an existing term name and date range
preconditions: An active term exists with known termId.
AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
steps: Call PATCH /v1/terms/{termId} with updated name and revised endDate within valid range.
Call GET /v1/terms/{termId} or filter GET /v1/terms to retrieve the updated record.
Verify linked class sections still reference the same termId.
cases.expected: Term name and date fields are updated in persistence; existing class sections remain bound to the same term without requiring re-creation.

cases.title: Deactivate term transitions isActive from true to false
preconditions: An active term with isActive true exists and has no blocking dependency policy in test fixture.
AcademicAdmin auth context is available.
steps: Record beforeState isActive true for the target term.
Invoke deactivate via PATCH /v1/terms/{termId} setting isActive false.
Query persistence and record afterState.
Call GET /v1/terms with active-only filter if supported and verify deactivated term exclusion from active term dropdown semantics.
cases.expected: beforeState isActive true transitions to afterState isActive false; deactivated term is excluded from active-term selectors used by class section create form (FRM-04).

cases.title: Non-admin roles are denied term management endpoints
preconditions: Student, Lecturer, and ITAdmin test accounts exist with valid authentication tokens.
At least one term exists in the system.
steps: Call POST /v1/terms as Student, Lecturer, and ITAdmin.
Call GET /v1/terms as Student.
Call PATCH /v1/terms/{termId} as Lecturer and ITAdmin.
cases.expected: All unauthorized role requests return 403 Forbidden or equivalent authorization error without exposing term management data or allowing mutations; no partial restricted datasets are returned.

cases.title: Term create rejects endDate before startDate with validation error
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
steps: Send POST /v1/terms with startDate 2026-12-31 and endDate 2026-08-01.
Read HTTP status and error.code from the response envelope.
Query terms table to confirm no row was created for the invalid payload.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 400 with InvalidPayload or equivalent validation error; no term row is persisted for the invalid date range.

cases.title: Duplicate term code on create returns conflict without silent overwrite
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
A term with code 2026-1 already exists in institution scope.
steps: Send POST /v1/terms with the same code 2026-1 and different name/dates.
Read HTTP status and error.code.
Verify the original term record is unchanged.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 409 or equivalent conflict error for duplicate code; existing term record remains unchanged with no silent overwrite.

cases.title: Academic Admin browser journey on PG-07 terms list and FRM-02 create form
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is logged in on desktop Chromium.
Admin setup navigation exposes Terms page at /admin/terms.
steps: Navigate to /admin/terms and verify TableToolbar and DataTable render with term columns (code, name, start date, end date, active status).
Click Create term and complete FRM-02 with valid code, name, dates, and active flag.
Submit the form and observe success feedback.
Confirm the new term row appears in the list without full page reload if SPA routing applies.
cases.expected: PG-07 displays paginated term list with expected columns; FRM-02 create flow succeeds and the new term is visible in the table with correct active badge/state.
